misconceptionSummary: Students multiply the known side by the area instead of dividing the area by the known side.

visuals.ruleText: Area = length × width   →   missing side = Area ÷ known side
visuals.subText: Divide the area by the side you know to find the side you don't

steps.narration: The area formula is length times width. To find a missing side, rearrange: divide the area by the side you already know. If area equals forty-eight and one side is six, the missing side is forty-eight divided by six.
Area is forty-eight square centimetres, width is six centimetres. Length equals forty-eight divided by six, which is eight centimetres.

lines.text: Missing side is always Area ÷ known side
Works for triangles too: base = 2 × Area ÷ height

steps.narration: Check: eight times six is forty-eight — correct. For triangles, the formula is half base times height, so rearrange differently: base equals two times the area divided by the height.
Eight centimetres. Rearrange the area formula by dividing — never multiply — the area by the known side.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: multiplying the known side by the area (6 × 48 = 288). The missing side is found by dividing: 48 ÷ 6 = 8 cm. Rearrange area = l × w to get l = area ÷ w.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: To find a missing side, divide the area by the side you know — not multiply. Area = length × width rearranges to length = area ÷ width.
